dependencies: High-quality arabica coffee beans from multiple coffee-producing regions
Dairy products particularly fluid milk and plant-based alternatives
Global Coffee Alliance with Nestlé for Channel Development distribution
Third-party suppliers for food products, paper and plastic products
Third-party logistic service providers and common carriers
risks: Coffee price volatility due to supply and demand factors in producing countries
Weather conditions, natural disasters, crop disease and pests affecting coffee supply
Climate change impacts on coffee production and water availability
Supplier failure to meet quality standards or comply with laws
Reliance on single or very few suppliers for certain products
Supply chain interruptions from casualty loss of roasting plants
Trade restrictions including tariffs, quotas, embargoes, customs restrictions
Pandemics, social or labor unrest, labor shortages affecting supply chain
Concentration of retail licensed operations in relatively small number of large licensees
Dependence on Nestlé performance for Channel Development business

regulatory_risks: Complex and evolving U.S. and international laws regarding data privacy and data protection (GDPR, PIPL, CCPA, California Privacy Rights Act)
State and federal legislation regarding wages, scheduling, and benefits
Increased minimum wage requirements (e.g., California Assembly Bill 1238)
Fair Labor Standards Act overtime requirements changes
Environmental laws and regulations including carbon emissions limits, plastic use restrictions, water use regulations
Import tariffs and restrictions based on alleged human rights abuses
Taxes on beverages with sugar or caloric sweeteners
OECD Pillar Two global minimum tax (15% on country-by-country basis)
European Union Corporate Sustainability Reporting Directive (CSRD)
Health and safety protocols and regulations
Zoning approvals and governmental permits for store openings

sanction_risks: Economic or trade sanctions affecting ability to source products or conduct business in certain markets
U.S.-China tensions including escalating tension, increased anti-Americanism, potential tariff increases, retaliations, restrictive regulations, or boycotts
Restrictive actions of foreign or U.S. governmental authorities affecting trade and foreign investment
Protective measures such as export and customs duties and tariffs, government intervention favoring local competitors
